discussion_comments.comment_body: Looks like `FontAwesomeIcon` is imported both here and down below.
Similar comment to above. Consider using `[...].join('/')`.

nit: I tend to prefer the use of `const` over `let` whenever possible because it makes code easier to read (as readers don't need to hunt down where/when/why the value might be re-assigned).

In this case, it seems pretty straightforward to use a `const` with a short ternary to avoid the reassignable variable:

```ts
const repository = curriculumLocale === 'english' ? 'freeCodeCamp' : 'i18n-curriculum';
```

Any chance we can make this a link that looks like a button instead?

It looks like that's [easily doable](https://github.com/freeCodeCamp/freeCodeCamp/blob/86f34c557af1239de2c0dd5a62857341edd94159/client/src/templates/Challenges/components/help-modal.tsx#L303).

My reasonining is that it's always better to render links as links and buttons as buttons. This helps with assistive technologies and also features like browsers' ability to copy a link's URL to the clipboard or open it in a new window/tab depending on the user's desire.
I think we have a component that does this exact thing?

nit: For this, it might be more readable if you remove some of the indirection and processing of static data:

```ts
[
  respository,
  'blob/main/curriculum/challenges',
  curriculumLocale,
  'blocks',
  block,
  `${challengeId}.md`,
].join('/'),
```

```suggestion
          onClick={() => updateIsClassroomAccount()}
```
If it `isClassroomAccount` the button is disabled, so you don't need the conditional.
